---
title: "Evaluating Batch Effects and Correction Quality with proBatch"
author: |
  | Yuliya Burankova, CoSyBio, University of Hamburg, Germany
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  BiocStyle::html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
bibliography: "references.bib"
csl: "nature-no-superscript.csl"
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Evaluating Batch Effects and Correction Quality with proBatch}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
  %\usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
editor_options:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---

```{r global-options, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
    echo = TRUE,
    warning = FALSE,
    message = FALSE,
    fig.crop = FALSE,
    fig.pos = "H",
    fig.align = "center",
    fig.width = 9,
    fig.height = 4.8,
    fig.wide = TRUE,
    out.width = "100%",
    collapse = TRUE,
    comment = "#>"
)

biocpkg <- function(pkg) {
    if (requireNamespace("BiocStyle", quietly = TRUE)) {
        as.character(BiocStyle::Biocpkg(pkg))
    } else {
        pkg
    }
}

safe_classification_metrics <- function(x,
                                        sample_annotation,
                                        known_col = "MS_batch",
                                        pbf_name = NULL,
                                        fill_the_missing = -1,
                                        ...) {
    known_cols <- unique(as.character(known_col))
    known_cols <- known_cols[!is.na(known_cols)]
    known_cols <- trimws(known_cols)
    known_cols <- known_cols[nzchar(known_cols)]

    if (is.null(sample_annotation) || !length(known_cols)) {
        stop("`sample_annotation` must contain the `known_col` used for classification metrics.")
    }

    missing_cols <- setdiff(known_cols, names(sample_annotation))
    if (length(missing_cols)) {
        stop(
            "`sample_annotation` must contain the `known_col` used for classification metrics. Missing: ",
            paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")
        )
    }

    valid_known_cols <- known_cols[vapply(
        known_cols,
        function(col_nm) {
            n_levels <- length(unique(sample_annotation[[col_nm]][!is.na(sample_annotation[[col_nm]])]))
            n_levels >= 2L
        },
        logical(1)
    )]
    skipped_known_cols <- setdiff(known_cols, valid_known_cols)
    if (length(skipped_known_cols)) {
        message(
            "Skipping classification metrics for: ",
            paste(skipped_known_cols, collapse = ", "),
            " (fewer than 2 observed levels in the selected subset)."
        )
    }
    if (!length(valid_known_cols)) {
        return(data.frame(
            known_col = known_cols,
            ARI = NA_real_,
            MCC = NA_real_,
            silhouette = NA_real_,
            stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        ))
    }
    if (inherits(x, "ProBatchFeatures")) {
        return(calculate_classification_metrics(
            x,
            sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
            known_col = valid_known_cols,
            pbf_name = pbf_name,
            fill_the_missing = fill_the_missing,
            ...
        ))
    }
    calculate_classification_metrics(
        x,
        sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
        known_col = valid_known_cols,
        fill_the_missing = fill_the_missing,
        ...
    )
}
```

# Introduction

This vignette focuses on evaluation metrics for batch effects in
`r biocpkg("proBatch")`, with an emphasis on interpreting diagnostics
before and after correction.

It complements:

- the main workflow vignette (`proBatch.Rmd`) for end-to-end correction
  strategy,
- the `ProBatchFeatures` vignette (`proBatchFeatures.Rmd`) for
  `QFeatures`-based data processing pipelines.


<div class="alert alert-info">
**Note:** If you are new to the package, start with `proBatch.Rmd` and
`proBatchFeatures.Rmd`, then return here for deeper diagnostic and
quality-control interpretation.
</div>

# Installation

Install the release version from Bioconductor:

```{r install-probatch, eval=FALSE}
# if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
#     install.packages("BiocManager")
# BiocManager::install("proBatch")
```

To install the development version from GitHub:

```{r install-probatch-devel, eval=FALSE}
if (!require("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages("remotes")
}
remotes::install_github("Freddsle/proBatch")
```

# Workflow Overview

The workflow used in this vignette is summarized in the table below and
follows the same high-level logic as the package overview vignette.

```{r workflow-overview-figure, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Batch-effect analysis workflow used across proBatch vignettes.", out.width="55%"}
knitr::include_graphics("Batch_effects_workflow_staircase.png")
```

```{r workflow-overview-table, echo=FALSE}
workflow_overview <- tibble::tibble(
    Stage = c(
        "1. Setup and data checks",
        "2. Baseline diagnostics on raw/log data",
        "3. Apply one correction method",
        "4. Repeat diagnostics on corrected data",
        "5. Summarize raw vs corrected outcomes"
    ),
    `Main functions` = c(
        "`ProBatchFeatures()`, `pb_transform()`, metadata checks",
        "Missingness, distributions, clustering, embeddings, PVCA, correlations, QC metrics",
        "`pb_transform(steps = \"limmaRBE\")`",
        "Same diagnostics to ensure apples-to-apples comparison",
        "Correlation overlays, PVCA stack, summary metric table"
    ),
    `Main decision` = c(
        "Is input design valid and analyzable?",
        "How strong are technical effects before correction?",
        "Which correction assumptions are being applied?",
        "Did technical variation decrease without harming biology?",
        "Is quality gain sufficient for downstream analysis?"
    )
)

knitr::kable(
    workflow_overview,
    caption = "Five-stage diagnostic workflow for evaluating batch correction quality."
)
```

# Data and Setup

## Load required packages

```{r load-packages, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(proBatch)

library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
```

## Load example data with `data()`

The package provides several example objects. Here we explicitly load
three objects:

- `example_proteome_matrix`: feature-by-sample intensity matrix,
- `example_sample_annotation`: sample-level technical and biological
  metadata,
- `example_peptide_annotation`: feature-to-protein/gene annotations.

```{r prepare-example-data}
set.seed(1)

# Explicitly load required example objects from proBatch.
data(list = c(
    "example_proteome_matrix",
    "example_sample_annotation",
    "example_peptide_annotation"
), package = "proBatch")

# Subset samples/features for computational efficiency.
# This keeps the vignette fast while retaining representative structure.
sample_ids <- example_sample_annotation$FullRunName[1:60]
sample_annotation <- example_sample_annotation[
    match(sample_ids, example_sample_annotation$FullRunName),
]

# Feature-level selectors for illustrative plots.
protein_name <- if ("Haao" %in% example_peptide_annotation$Gene) {
    "Haao"
} else {
    unique(example_peptide_annotation$Gene)[1]
}

spike_in_name <- if ("BOVINE_A1ag" %in% example_peptide_annotation$Gene) {
    "BOVINE_A1ag"
} else {
    unique(example_peptide_annotation$Gene)[1]
}

irt_pattern <- if (any(grepl("iRT", example_peptide_annotation$ProteinName))) {
    "iRT"
} else {
    example_peptide_annotation$ProteinName[1]
}

peptides_from_protein <- example_peptide_annotation$peptide_group_label[
    example_peptide_annotation$Gene %in% protein_name
]
peptides_from_spike <- example_peptide_annotation$peptide_group_label[
    example_peptide_annotation$Gene %in% spike_in_name
]
peptides_from_irt <- example_peptide_annotation$peptide_group_label[
    grepl(irt_pattern, example_peptide_annotation$ProteinName)
]

feature_ids <- unique(c(
    rownames(example_proteome_matrix)[1:200],
    peptides_from_protein,
    peptides_from_spike,
    peptides_from_irt
))
feature_ids <- intersect(feature_ids, rownames(example_proteome_matrix))

# Raw matrix and annotations.
data_matrix_raw <- example_proteome_matrix[feature_ids, sample_ids]

peptide_annotation <- example_peptide_annotation[
    match(feature_ids, example_peptide_annotation$peptide_group_label),
]
peptide_annotation <- peptide_annotation[!is.na(peptide_annotation$peptide_group_label), ]
rownames(peptide_annotation) <- peptide_annotation$peptide_group_label

# Primary analysis container: ProBatchFeatures (QFeatures-based).
pbf_raw <- ProBatchFeatures(
    data_matrix = data_matrix_raw,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    sample_id_col = "FullRunName",
    name = "feature::raw"
)

# Log-transform for diagnostics that assume approximate homoscedasticity.
pbf_log <- pb_transform(
    pbf_raw,
    from = "feature::raw",
    steps = "log2",
    store_fast_steps = TRUE
)

# Matrix/long views derived from PBF for helpers requiring explicit formats.
data_matrix_raw_log <- pb_assay_matrix(pbf_log)
df_long_raw <- pb_as_long(
    pbf_log,
    feature_id_col = "peptide_group_label",
    sample_id_col = "FullRunName",
    measure_col = "Intensity"
)

samples_for_corr <- sample_ids[1:12]

pvca_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "pvca_eval")
pvca_raw_dir <- file.path(pvca_dir, "raw")
pvca_corr_dir <- file.path(pvca_dir, "limmaRBE")

# Consistent colors for sample annotations.
color_list <- sample_annotation_to_colors(
    pbf_log,
    factor_columns = c("MS_batch", "Diet", "Sex", "Strain", "digestion_batch"),
    numeric_columns = c("DateTime", "order")
)

feature_id <- "10231_QDVDVWLWQQEGSSK_2"
if (!feature_id %in% feature_ids) {
    feature_id <- feature_ids[1]
}
```

<div class="alert alert-warning">
**Warning:** This vignette intentionally subsets to 60 samples and ~200+
selected features for speed. For real analyses, run diagnostics on all
samples/features (or a principled stratified subset) before making
correction decisions.
</div>

# Correction Methods Available in proBatch

This vignette applies one reference method (`limmaRBE`) for a full
before/after demonstration. The table below summarizes correction
methods already available in `r biocpkg("proBatch")`.

```{r correction-methods-table, echo=FALSE}
correction_methods <- tibble::tibble(
    `Method family` = c(
        "limma removeBatchEffect",
        "ComBat",
        "mComBat",
        "Feature-wise centering",
        "Continuous drift + discrete step",
        "BERT-based correction (optional)",
        "(s)PLSDA-batch (optional)",
        "RUV-III-C (optional)",
        "NormAE (optional)",
        "omicsGMF correction (optional)",
        "Unified correction wrapper"
    ),
    `Function / step` = c(
        "`correct_with_removeBatchEffect()` / `pb_transform(steps = \"limmaRBE\")`",
        "`correct_with_ComBat()` / `pb_transform(steps = \"combat\")`",
        "`correct_with_mComBat()` / `pb_transform(steps = \"mComBat\")`",
        "`center_feature_batch()` or `correct_batch_effects(discrete_func = ...)`",
        "`adjust_batch_trend_df()` or `adjust_batch_trend_dm()` then discrete correction",
        "`correct_with_BERT()` / `pb_transform(steps = \"BERT\", params_list = list(list(bert_method = ...)))`",
        "`correct_with_PLSDA_batch()` / `pb_transform(steps = \"PLSDAbatch\")` or `\"sPLSDAbatch\"`",
        "`correct_with_RUVIII_C()` / `pb_transform(steps = \"RUVIIIC\")` (alias: `\"RUVIII_C\"`)",
        "`correct_with_NormAE()` / `pb_transform(steps = \"NormAE\")` (alias: `\"normae\"`)",
        "`correct_with_omicsGMF()` / `pb_transform(steps = \"omicsGMFcor\")` (alias: `\"omicsgmfcor\"`)",
        "`correct_batch_effects()` / `correct_batch_effects_df()` / `correct_batch_effects_dm()`"
    ),
    `Missing-value behavior` = c(
        "Matrix NAs are allowed; batch/covariate design columns must be complete",
        "Requires NA-free matrix",
        "Requires NA-free matrix and chosen reference center",
        "Can work with missing values depending on method",
        "Trend fitting can tolerate missingness; discrete step dictates final constraints",
        "Designed to tolerate missing values",
        "Requires NA handling or imputation policy as configured",
        "Designed to handle missingness with feature-wise normalization strategy",
        "Requires NA handling before correction (unless preprocessed)",
        "Can model missingness internally within the omicsGMF workflow",
        "Depends on selected continuous/discrete method(s)"
    ),
    `Typical use` = c(
        "Linear discrete batch effects while preserving design covariates",
        "Strong discrete batch offsets with empirical Bayes shrinkage",
        "ComBat variant with explicit center batch",
        "Simple robust per-feature shift alignment",
        "Run-order drift plus discrete batch differences",
        "Complex nonlinear effects; tree-based decomposition",
        "Latent-component correction preserving treatment-associated structure",
        "Control-feature and replicate-driven normalization for unwanted variation",
        "Autoencoder-based nonlinear correction using external Python model",
        "Latent factor model-based correction integrated with GMF workflow",
        "Single-call orchestration of continuous and discrete correction steps"
    ),
    `Availability` = c(
        "Core",
        "Core",
        "Core",
        "Core",
        "Core",
        "Optional (BERT)",
        "Optional (PLSDAbatch)",
        "Optional (RUVIIIC)",
        "Optional (reticulate + Python NormAE)",
        "Optional (omicsGMF + sgdGMF)",
        "Core"
    )
)

knitr::kable(
    correction_methods,
    caption = "Batch-correction methods available in proBatch (core and optional)."
)
```

See function documentation for method-specific details:
`?correct_with_removeBatchEffect`, `?correct_with_ComBat`,
`?correct_with_mComBat`, `?center_feature_batch`,
`?adjust_batch_trend_df`, `?correct_with_BERT`,
`?correct_with_PLSDA_batch`, `?correct_with_RUVIII_C`,
`?correct_with_NormAE`, `?correct_with_omicsGMF`, and
`?correct_batch_effects`.

Imputation methods (for example `PRONEImpute`, `MFimpute`, and
`omicsGMFImpute`) are available in `proBatch`, but are not correction
methods and are therefore listed separately from this table.

For `BERT` in `pb_transform()`, the stored assay names are tagged by
`bert_method` (for example `BERTc`, `BERTl`, `BERTr`). Deprecated
compatibility wrappers (for example `*_df`/`*_dm` legacy variants) are
not listed separately because they map to the same correction families.

# Baseline Diagnostics on Raw Data

This section establishes a pre-correction baseline. Use it to identify
whether technical effects dominate and which correction assumptions are
reasonable.

## Metadata and design checks

- Purpose: verify metadata integrity, sample alignment, factor typing,
  and design confounding.
- Interpretation: design warnings, duplicated columns, or nested
  structures indicate potential invalid modeling assumptions.
- Expected patterns: complete sample mapping and non-confounded
  `MS_batch`/biological factors.
- Action items: fix metadata first; do not run correction on inconsistent
  annotation.

```{r raw-metadata-design-checks}
# Metadata quality and design checks.
meta_summary_raw <- metadata_column_summary(pbf_log)
dup_cols_raw <- find_duplicated_columns(pbf_log)

# Example duplicated column handling.
sample_annotation_dups <- sample_annotation %>%
    mutate(Diet_copy = Diet)

dup_cols_dups <- find_duplicated_columns(sample_annotation_dups)
filtered_meta <- filter_metadata_columns(
    sample_annotation_dups,
    duplicate_keep = "pattern",
    duplicate_pattern = "_copy$"
)

warn_unmapped_columns(sample_annotation, c("MS_batch", "Diet"), "FullRunName")

# Factor/numeric overlap and automatic type guessing.
overlap_factors <- handle_factor_numeric_overlap(
    factor_columns = c("MS_batch", "Diet", "order"),
    numeric_columns = c("order")
)

guessed_cols <- guess_factor_columns_if_needed(
    factor_columns = c("MS_batch", "order"),
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    guess_factors = TRUE
)

converted_meta <- convert_annotation_classes(
    sample_annotation,
    factor_columns = c("MS_batch", "Diet", "Sex"),
    numeric_columns = c("DateTime", "order")
)

# Collapse rare factor levels.
merged_strain <- merge_rare_levels(sample_annotation$Strain, rare_thr = 2)

# Ensure sample IDs match between annotation and data.
df_long_checked_raw <- check_sample_consistency(
    sample_annotation,
    sample_id_col = "FullRunName",
    df_long = df_long_raw,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    order_col = "order"
)

design_check_raw <- validate_batch_design(
    sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    condition_col = "Diet",
    covariates = c("Sex", "Strain"),
    sample_id_col = "FullRunName",
    strict = FALSE
)

design_summary_raw <- summarize_design(
    sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    condition_col = "Diet",
    covariates = c("Sex", "Strain")
)

# Identify nested batch structures.
nested_raw <- detect_nested_batches(
    sample_annotation,
    batch_cols = c("MS_batch", "digestion_batch", "Diet")
)

# Inspect metadata summaries and flags.
meta_summary_raw
dup_cols_raw
dup_cols_dups
head(filtered_meta)
overlap_factors
guessed_cols
head(converted_meta)
head(merged_strain)
head(df_long_checked_raw)
design_check_raw
design_summary_raw
nested_raw
```

## Missingness patterns

- Purpose: quantify NA structure across samples/features and identify
  batch-associated missingness.
- Interpretation: horizontal/vertical NA bands linked to `MS_batch`
  often indicate technical causes.
- Expected patterns: missingness may exist, but should not be strongly
  batch-structured after correction.
- Action items: consider missing-value handling strategy before methods
  that require complete matrices (for example ComBat).

```{r raw-missingness-diagnostics, fig.cap="Raw-data missingness diagnostics across samples and features (heatmap, density, and frequency summaries).", fig.height=4.5}
# Missing-value diagnostics.
plot_NA_heatmap(
    pbf_raw,
    color_by = "MS_batch",
    label_by = "FullRunName",
    cluster_samples = FALSE,
    cluster_features = TRUE,
    main = "Raw data: missing-value heatmap"
)

plot_NA_density(pbf_raw) +
    ggtitle("Raw data: intensity density by missingness")

plot_NA_frequency(pbf_raw) +
    ggtitle("Raw data: missing-value frequency")
```

## Sample distributions

- Purpose: assess global abundance shifts and spread differences between
  samples/batches.
- Interpretation: consistent vertical offsets by batch suggest
  systematic technical bias.
- Expected patterns: correction should reduce batch-specific offsets
  without flattening biological structure.
- Action items: if global offsets dominate, include normalization and/or
  discrete batch adjustment.

```{r raw-sample-distributions, fig.cap="Raw-data sample distributions: sample means over run order and per-sample boxplots colored by batch.", fig.height=5.2}
plot_sample_mean(
    pbf_log,
    order_col = "order",
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    color_by_batch = TRUE,
    color_scheme = color_list,
    plot_title = "Raw data: sample mean over run order"
)

plot_boxplot(
    pbf_log,
    sample_id_col = "FullRunName",
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    color_by_batch = TRUE,
    color_scheme = color_list,
    plot_title = "Raw data: per-sample intensity distributions"
)
```

## Heatmaps and clustering

- Purpose: evaluate whether samples cluster by technical or biological
  factors.
- Interpretation: dominant clustering by `MS_batch` indicates batch
  structure likely driving downstream analyses.
- Expected patterns: after successful correction, batch-driven grouping
  weakens and biological grouping becomes more apparent.
- Action items: if clustering is overwhelmingly technical, prioritize
  correction before differential analyses.

```{r raw-heatmap-clustering, fig.cap="Raw-data structure diagnostics via heatmaps and hierarchical clustering with sample annotations.", fig.height=5.2}
plot_heatmap_diagnostic(
    pbf_log,
    factors_to_plot = c("MS_batch", "Diet", "Sex"),
    color_list = color_list,
    show_rownames = FALSE,
    show_colnames = FALSE
)

plot_heatmap_generic(
    pbf_log,
    column_annotation_df = sample_annotation,
    row_annotation_df = peptide_annotation,
    col_ann_id_col = "FullRunName",
    row_ann_id_col = "peptide_group_label",
    columns_for_cols = c("MS_batch", "Diet"),
    columns_for_rows = c("Gene"),
    annotation_color_cols = color_list,
    plot_title = "Raw data: generic heatmap"
)

plot_hierarchical_clustering(
    pbf_log,
    factors_to_plot = c("MS_batch", "Diet", "Sex"),
    color_list = color_list,
    label_samples = FALSE,
    plot_title = "Raw data: hierarchical clustering"
)
```

## Embeddings (PCA, t-SNE, UMAP)

- Purpose: visualize sample structure in reduced dimensions.
- Interpretation: separation dominated by `MS_batch` in low-dimensional
  space indicates unresolved technical variance.
- Expected patterns: correction should reduce batch separation while
  preserving biologically meaningful separation.
- Action items: adjust correction covariates and reassess if technical
  axes still dominate.

```{r raw-embeddings, cache=TRUE, fig.cap="Raw-data low-dimensional embeddings (PCA, t-SNE, UMAP) colored by MS batch.", fig.height=4.8}
pca_raw <- plot_PCA(
    pbf_log,
    color_by = "MS_batch",
    shape_by = "Diet",
    plot_title = "PCA (raw)"
)
pca_raw

tsne_raw <- plot_TSNE(
    pbf_log,
    color_by = "MS_batch",
    perplexity = 10,
    max_iter = 300,
    initial_dims = 10,
    plot_title = "t-SNE (raw)"
)
tsne_raw

umap_raw <- plot_UMAP(
    pbf_log,
    color_by = "MS_batch",
    n_neighbors = 10,
    min_dist = 0.3,
    random_state = 1,
    plot_title = "UMAP (raw)"
)
umap_raw
```

## Variance decomposition (PVCA and variance partition)

- Purpose: quantify explained variance attributable to technical and
  biological factors.
- Interpretation: high technical contributions (for example `MS_batch`)
  indicate substantial residual batch effects.
- Expected patterns: technical variance should decrease after correction;
  key biological factors should remain interpretable.
- Action items: compare both PVCA and per-feature variance partition to
  avoid over-reliance on one summary.

```{r raw-pvca, fig.cap="Raw-data PVCA summaries showing weighted variance contributions across technical and biological factors.", fig.height=4.8}
pvca_raw <- calculate_PVCA(
    pbf_log,
    factors_for_PVCA = c("MS_batch", "Diet", "Sex", "Strain"),
    fill_the_missing = -1
)

plot_PVCA(
    pbf_log,
    technical_factors = c("MS_batch", "digestion_batch"),
    biological_factors = c("Diet", "Sex", "Strain"),
    fill_the_missing = -1,
    plot_title = "PVCA (raw)"
)

pvca_df_raw <- prepare_PVCA_df(
    pbf_log,
    pbf_name = pb_current_assay(pbf_log),
    technical_factors = c("MS_batch", "digestion_batch"),
    biological_factors = c("Diet", "Sex", "Strain"),
    fill_the_missing = -1,
    variance_threshold = 0.05,
    path_to_save_results = pvca_raw_dir
)

plot_PVCA.df(pvca_df_raw, plot_title = "PVCA (raw, prepared)")

pvca_raw
```

```{r raw-variance-partition, cache=TRUE, fig.cap="Raw-data variance partitioning across modeled factors, including per-feature variance summaries.", fig.height=4.8}
vp_raw <- calculate_variance_partition(
    pbf_log,
    model_variables = c("MS_batch", "Diet", "Sex"),
    fill_the_missing = -1
)

plot_variance_partition(
    pbf_log,
    technical_factors = "MS_batch",
    biological_factors = c("Diet", "Sex"),
    variance_threshold = 0.05,
    summary_stat = "median",
    plot_title = "Variance partition (raw)"
)

vp_df_raw <- prepare_variance_partition_df(
    pbf_log,
    pbf_name = pb_current_assay(pbf_log),
    technical_factors = "MS_batch",
    biological_factors = c("Diet", "Sex"),
    variance_threshold = 0.05,
    abundance_top_n = 20,
    abundance_direction = "most"
)

if (is.list(vp_df_raw) && !is.data.frame(vp_df_raw)) {
    current_assay <- pb_current_assay(pbf_log)
    if (!is.null(names(vp_df_raw)) && current_assay %in% names(vp_df_raw)) {
        vp_df_raw <- vp_df_raw[[current_assay]]
    } else {
        vp_df_raw <- vp_df_raw[[1]]
    }
}

plot_variance_partition.df(vp_df_raw, plot_title = "Variance partition (raw, prepared)")

head(vp_raw)
```

## Correlation analyses

- Purpose: assess sample reproducibility and peptide/protein coherence.
- Interpretation: if within-batch similarity dominates over within-
  biospecimen similarity, technical bias is likely inflating
  correlations.
- Expected patterns: post-correction, within-biospecimen correlations
  should improve relative to technical groupings.
- Action items: inspect both matrix heatmaps and distribution summaries.

```{r raw-corr-heatmaps, fig.cap="Raw-data correlation heatmaps for selected samples and protein-specific peptide sets.", fig.height=5.2}
corr_matrix_raw <- cor(
    data_matrix_raw_log[, samples_for_corr, drop = FALSE],
    use = "pairwise.complete.obs"
)

plot_corr_matrix(
    corr_matrix_raw,
    annotation = sample_annotation,
    annotation_id_col = "FullRunName",
    factors_to_plot = c("MS_batch", "Diet"),
    plot_title = "Raw data: sample correlation matrix"
)

plot_sample_corr_heatmap(
    pbf_log,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    samples_to_plot = samples_for_corr,
    factors_to_plot = c("MS_batch", "Diet"),
    cluster_rows = TRUE,
    cluster_cols = TRUE,
    plot_title = "Raw data: sample correlation heatmap"
)

plot_protein_corrplot(
    pbf_log,
    protein_name = protein_name,
    peptide_annotation = peptide_annotation,
    protein_col = "Gene",
    cluster_rows = TRUE,
    cluster_cols = TRUE,
    plot_title = sprintf("Raw data: peptide correlation for %s", protein_name)
)
```

```{r raw-corr-distributions, fig.cap="Raw-data correlation distributions for sample pairs and peptide pairs.", fig.height=4.8}
sample_corr_df_raw <- calculate_sample_corr_distr(
    pbf_log,
    sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    biospecimen_id_col = "EarTag"
)

plot_sample_corr_distribution(
    pbf_log,
    sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    biospecimen_id_col = "EarTag",
    plot_param = "batch_replicate",
    plot_title = "Raw data: sample correlation by pair type"
)

plot_sample_corr_distribution.corrDF(
    sample_corr_df_raw,
    plot_param = "batch_replicate",
    plot_title = "Raw data: sample correlation (from precomputed table)"
)

peptide_corr_df_raw <- calculate_peptide_corr_distr(
    pbf_log,
    peptide_annotation,
    protein_col = "Gene"
)

plot_peptide_corr_distribution(
    pbf_log,
    peptide_annotation,
    protein_col = "Gene",
    plot_title = "Raw data: peptide correlation distribution"
)

plot_peptide_corr_distribution.corrDF(
    peptide_corr_df_raw,
    plot_title = "Raw data: peptide correlation (from precomputed table)"
)

head(sample_corr_df_raw)
head(peptide_corr_df_raw)
```

## Feature-level diagnostics

- Purpose: inspect specific peptides/proteins/spike-ins/iRT traces for
  local artifacts and drift.
- Interpretation: strong run-order trends or batch jumps in controls
  imply technical drift not addressed by global summaries.
- Expected patterns: spike-in/iRT profiles should be stable across runs;
  fitted drift curves should flatten after correction.
- Action items: include trend correction (`adjust_batch_trend_*`) when
  run-order artifacts are substantial.

```{r raw-feature-diagnostics, fig.cap="Raw-data feature-level diagnostics for selected peptides/proteins, controls, and fitted drift curves.", fig.height=5.2}
plot_single_feature(
    feature_name = feature_id,
    df_long = pbf_log,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    plot_title = sprintf("Raw data: feature profile (%s)", feature_id)
)

plot_peptides_of_one_protein(
    protein_name = protein_name,
    peptide_annotation = peptide_annotation,
    protein_col = "Gene",
    df_long = pbf_log,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    plot_title = sprintf("Raw data: peptides from %s", protein_name)
)

plot_spike_in(
    spike_ins = spike_in_name,
    peptide_annotation = peptide_annotation,
    protein_col = "Gene",
    df_long = pbf_log,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    plot_title = sprintf("Raw data: spike-in profile (%s)", spike_in_name)
)

plot_iRT(
    irt_pattern = irt_pattern,
    peptide_annotation = peptide_annotation,
    protein_col = "ProteinName",
    df_long = pbf_log,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    plot_title = sprintf("Raw data: iRT profile (%s)", irt_pattern)
)

fit_df_raw <- adjust_batch_trend_df(
    pbf_log,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    span = 0.7
)
fit_df_raw <- fit_df_raw[fit_df_raw$peptide_group_label %in% feature_id, ]

plot_with_fitting_curve(
    feature_name = feature_id,
    df_long = pbf_log,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    fit_df = fit_df_raw,
    plot_title = sprintf("Raw data: fitted trend (%s)", feature_id)
)
```

## QC summaries

- Purpose: aggregate correction-relevant quality metrics (CV,
  clustering-agreement, intragroup stability, outliers, and sub-batches).
- Interpretation: lower feature CVs, fewer outliers, weaker batch
  clustering scores, and reduced sub-batch artifacts are generally
  favorable.
- Expected patterns: post-correction metrics should improve or remain
  stable without biological collapse.
- Action items: use these metrics jointly; no single threshold should be
  used in isolation.

```{r raw-qc-summaries, cache=TRUE, fig.cap="Raw-data QC summaries: CV distributions, clustering metrics, intragroup variation, and outlier/sub-batch diagnostics.", fig.height=4.8}
cv_df_raw <- calculate_feature_CV(
    pbf_log,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    biospecimen_id_col = "EarTag",
    unlog = FALSE
)

cv_df_raw_plot <- cv_df_raw %>%
    filter(is.finite(CV_total))

if (nrow(cv_df_raw_plot) == 0) {
    message("Skipping CV plots (raw): no features with sufficient measurements.")
} else {
    plot_CV_distr.df(cv_df_raw_plot, batch_col = "MS_batch", plot_title = "Raw data: CV distribution")
    plot_CV_distr.df(cv_df_raw_plot, batch_col = "MS_batch", plot_title = "CV distribution (raw)")
}

class_metrics_raw <- safe_classification_metrics(
    pbf_log,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    known_col = "MS_batch"
)

intragroup_raw <- plot_intragroup_variation(
    pbf_log,
    group_col = "Diet",
    fill_the_missing = -1,
    metrics = "correlation"
)

outliers_raw <- detect_outlier_samples(
    pbf_log,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    n_pcs = 5
)

subbatches_raw <- subbatch_detection(
    pbf_log,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    n_pcs = 5,
    method = "kmeans",
    k_max = 4
)

head(cv_df_raw)
class_metrics_raw
outliers_raw
subbatches_raw$summary
```

# Apply a Reference Correction Method

We now apply one correction method (`limma::removeBatchEffect`) through
`pb_transform()` using step `"limmaRBE"`.

This section demonstrates a single method in depth; see
[Correction Methods Available in proBatch](#correction-methods-available-in-probatch)
for alternatives.

```{r apply-limma-rbe}
# limma::removeBatchEffect requires at least two observed batch levels.
batch_levels <- unique(sample_annotation$MS_batch[!is.na(sample_annotation$MS_batch)])
limma_covariates <- c("Diet", "Sex")
limma_covariates <- limma_covariates[limma_covariates %in% names(sample_annotation)]
limma_covariates <- limma_covariates[vapply(limma_covariates, function(col) {
    length(unique(sample_annotation[[col]][!is.na(sample_annotation[[col]])])) >= 2
}, logical(1))]

if (length(batch_levels) < 2L) {
    message("Skipping limmaRBE: MS_batch has fewer than 2 observed levels in this subset.")
    pbf_corr <- pbf_log
} else {
    limma_params <- list(batch_col = "MS_batch")
    if (length(limma_covariates)) {
        limma_params$covariates_cols <- limma_covariates
    }

    pbf_corr <- pb_transform(
        pbf_log,
        from = pb_current_assay(pbf_log),
        steps = "limmaRBE",
        params_list = list(limma_params)
    )
}

data_matrix_corrected <- pb_assay_matrix(pbf_corr)

# Long-format table for functions that explicitly consume long data.
df_long_corrected <- pb_as_long(
    pbf_corr,
    feature_id_col = "peptide_group_label",
    sample_id_col = "FullRunName",
    measure_col = "Intensity"
)
```

# Diagnostics After Correction

The same diagnostics are repeated after correction to support direct
comparison with the raw baseline.

## Metadata and design checks

Metadata and experimental design do not change after correction, but we
rerun checks for complete reporting and pipeline traceability.

```{r corr-metadata-design-checks}
meta_summary_corr <- metadata_column_summary(pbf_corr)
dup_cols_corr <- find_duplicated_columns(pbf_corr)

dup_cols_dups_corr <- find_duplicated_columns(sample_annotation_dups)
filtered_meta_corr <- filter_metadata_columns(
    sample_annotation_dups,
    duplicate_keep = "pattern",
    duplicate_pattern = "_copy$"
)

warn_unmapped_columns(sample_annotation, c("MS_batch", "Diet"), "FullRunName")

overlap_factors_corr <- handle_factor_numeric_overlap(
    factor_columns = c("MS_batch", "Diet", "order"),
    numeric_columns = c("order")
)

guessed_cols_corr <- guess_factor_columns_if_needed(
    factor_columns = c("MS_batch", "order"),
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    guess_factors = TRUE
)

converted_meta_corr <- convert_annotation_classes(
    sample_annotation,
    factor_columns = c("MS_batch", "Diet", "Sex"),
    numeric_columns = c("DateTime", "order")
)

merged_strain_corr <- merge_rare_levels(sample_annotation$Strain, rare_thr = 2)

df_long_checked_corr <- check_sample_consistency(
    sample_annotation,
    sample_id_col = "FullRunName",
    df_long = df_long_corrected,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    order_col = "order"
)

design_check_corr <- validate_batch_design(
    sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    condition_col = "Diet",
    covariates = c("Sex", "Strain"),
    sample_id_col = "FullRunName",
    strict = FALSE
)

design_summary_corr <- summarize_design(
    sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    condition_col = "Diet",
    covariates = c("Sex", "Strain")
)

nested_corr <- detect_nested_batches(
    sample_annotation,
    batch_cols = c("MS_batch", "digestion_batch", "Diet")
)

meta_summary_corr
dup_cols_corr
dup_cols_dups_corr
head(filtered_meta_corr)
overlap_factors_corr
guessed_cols_corr
head(converted_meta_corr)
head(merged_strain_corr)
head(df_long_checked_corr)
design_check_corr
design_summary_corr
nested_corr
```

## Missingness patterns

Interpretation follows the raw-data section: correction should generally
not create new missingness artifacts.

```{r corr-missingness-diagnostics, fig.cap="Post-correction missingness diagnostics to verify that NA structure remains stable after limmaRBE.", fig.height=4.5}
plot_NA_heatmap(
    pbf_corr,
    color_by = "MS_batch",
    label_by = "FullRunName",
    cluster_samples = FALSE,
    cluster_features = TRUE,
    main = "Corrected data: missing-value heatmap"
)

plot_NA_density(pbf_corr) +
    ggtitle("Corrected data: intensity density by missingness")

plot_NA_frequency(pbf_corr) +
    ggtitle("Corrected data: missing-value frequency")
```

## Sample distributions

Expectation: global batch offsets should be reduced compared with
Section [Sample distributions](#sample-distributions).

```{r corr-sample-distributions, fig.cap="Post-correction sample means and boxplots used to assess reduction of batch-driven global shifts.", fig.height=5.2}
plot_sample_mean(
    pbf_corr,
    order_col = "order",
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    color_by_batch = TRUE,
    color_scheme = color_list,
    plot_title = "Corrected data: sample mean over run order"
)

plot_boxplot(
    pbf_corr,
    sample_id_col = "FullRunName",
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    color_by_batch = TRUE,
    color_scheme = color_list,
    plot_title = "Corrected data: per-sample intensity distributions"
)
```

## Heatmaps and clustering

Expectation: clustering should shift away from technical factor dominance.

```{r corr-heatmap-clustering, fig.cap="Post-correction heatmaps and hierarchical clustering for residual batch-structure assessment.", fig.height=5.2}
plot_heatmap_diagnostic(
    pbf_corr,
    factors_to_plot = c("MS_batch", "Diet", "Sex"),
    color_list = color_list,
    show_rownames = FALSE,
    show_colnames = FALSE
)

plot_heatmap_generic(
    pbf_corr,
    column_annotation_df = sample_annotation,
    row_annotation_df = peptide_annotation,
    col_ann_id_col = "FullRunName",
    row_ann_id_col = "peptide_group_label",
    columns_for_cols = c("MS_batch", "Diet"),
    columns_for_rows = c("Gene"),
    annotation_color_cols = color_list,
    plot_title = "Generic heatmap (limmaRBE)"
)

plot_hierarchical_clustering(
    pbf_corr,
    factors_to_plot = c("MS_batch", "Diet", "Sex"),
    color_list = color_list,
    label_samples = FALSE,
    plot_title = "Corrected data: hierarchical clustering"
)
```

## Embeddings (PCA, t-SNE, UMAP)

Expectation: batch-separation structure should be weaker than in raw
embeddings.

```{r corr-embeddings, cache=TRUE, fig.cap="Post-correction low-dimensional embeddings for visual comparison against raw structure.", fig.height=4.8}
pca_corr <- plot_PCA(
    pbf_corr,
    color_by = "MS_batch",
    shape_by = "Diet",
    plot_title = "PCA (limmaRBE)"
)
pca_corr

tsne_corr <- plot_TSNE(
    pbf_corr,
    color_by = "MS_batch",
    perplexity = 10,
    max_iter = 300,
    initial_dims = 10,
    plot_title = "t-SNE (limmaRBE)"
)
tsne_corr

umap_corr <- plot_UMAP(
    pbf_corr,
    color_by = "MS_batch",
    n_neighbors = 10,
    min_dist = 0.3,
    random_state = 1,
    plot_title = "UMAP (limmaRBE)"
)
umap_corr
```

## Variance decomposition (PVCA and variance partition)

Expectation: technical variance (especially `MS_batch`) should decrease.

```{r corr-pvca, fig.cap="Post-correction PVCA summaries showing remaining technical and biological variance contributions.", fig.height=4.8}
pvca_corr <- calculate_PVCA(
    pbf_corr,
    factors_for_PVCA = c("MS_batch", "Diet", "Sex", "Strain"),
    fill_the_missing = -1
)

plot_PVCA(
    pbf_corr,
    technical_factors = c("MS_batch", "digestion_batch"),
    biological_factors = c("Diet", "Sex", "Strain"),
    fill_the_missing = -1,
    plot_title = "PVCA (limmaRBE)"
)

pvca_df_corr <- prepare_PVCA_df(
    pbf_corr,
    pbf_name = pb_current_assay(pbf_corr),
    technical_factors = c("MS_batch", "digestion_batch"),
    biological_factors = c("Diet", "Sex", "Strain"),
    fill_the_missing = -1,
    variance_threshold = 0.05,
    path_to_save_results = pvca_corr_dir
)

plot_PVCA.df(pvca_df_corr, plot_title = "PVCA (limmaRBE, prepared)")

pvca_corr
```

```{r corr-variance-partition, cache=TRUE, fig.cap="Post-correction variance partitioning used to compare per-feature explained variance before and after correction.", fig.height=4.8}
vp_corr <- calculate_variance_partition(
    pbf_corr,
    model_variables = c("MS_batch", "Diet", "Sex"),
    fill_the_missing = -1
)

plot_variance_partition(
    pbf_corr,
    technical_factors = "MS_batch",
    biological_factors = c("Diet", "Sex"),
    variance_threshold = 0.05,
    summary_stat = "median",
    plot_title = "Variance partition (limmaRBE)"
)

vp_df_corr <- prepare_variance_partition_df(
    pbf_corr,
    pbf_name = pb_current_assay(pbf_corr),
    technical_factors = "MS_batch",
    biological_factors = c("Diet", "Sex"),
    variance_threshold = 0.05,
    abundance_top_n = 20,
    abundance_direction = "most"
)

if (is.list(vp_df_corr) && !is.data.frame(vp_df_corr)) {
    current_assay <- pb_current_assay(pbf_corr)
    if (!is.null(names(vp_df_corr)) && current_assay %in% names(vp_df_corr)) {
        vp_df_corr <- vp_df_corr[[current_assay]]
    } else {
        vp_df_corr <- vp_df_corr[[1]]
    }
}

plot_variance_partition.df(vp_df_corr, plot_title = "Variance partition (limmaRBE, prepared)")

head(vp_corr)
```

## Correlation analyses

Expectation: sample correlations should better reflect biospecimen
consistency than batch identity.

```{r corr-corr-heatmaps, fig.cap="Post-correction correlation heatmaps for sample and peptide/protein-level structure checks.", fig.height=5.2}
corr_matrix_corr <- cor(
    data_matrix_corrected[, samples_for_corr, drop = FALSE],
    use = "pairwise.complete.obs"
)

plot_corr_matrix(
    corr_matrix_corr,
    annotation = sample_annotation,
    annotation_id_col = "FullRunName",
    factors_to_plot = c("MS_batch", "Diet"),
    plot_title = "Corrected data: sample correlation matrix"
)

plot_sample_corr_heatmap(
    pbf_corr,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    samples_to_plot = samples_for_corr,
    factors_to_plot = c("MS_batch", "Diet"),
    cluster_rows = TRUE,
    cluster_cols = TRUE,
    plot_title = "Corrected data: sample correlation heatmap"
)

plot_protein_corrplot(
    pbf_corr,
    protein_name = protein_name,
    peptide_annotation = peptide_annotation,
    protein_col = "Gene",
    cluster_rows = TRUE,
    cluster_cols = TRUE,
    plot_title = sprintf("Corrected data: peptide correlation for %s", protein_name)
)
```

```{r corr-corr-distributions, fig.cap="Post-correction correlation distributions across sample-pair and peptide-pair categories.", fig.height=4.8}
sample_corr_df_corr <- calculate_sample_corr_distr(
    pbf_corr,
    sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    biospecimen_id_col = "EarTag"
)

plot_sample_corr_distribution(
    pbf_corr,
    sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    biospecimen_id_col = "EarTag",
    plot_param = "batch_replicate",
    plot_title = "Corrected data: sample correlation by pair type"
)

plot_sample_corr_distribution.corrDF(
    sample_corr_df_corr,
    plot_param = "batch_replicate",
    plot_title = "Corrected data: sample correlation (from precomputed table)"
)

peptide_corr_df_corr <- calculate_peptide_corr_distr(
    pbf_corr,
    peptide_annotation,
    protein_col = "Gene"
)

plot_peptide_corr_distribution(
    pbf_corr,
    peptide_annotation,
    protein_col = "Gene",
    plot_title = "Corrected data: peptide correlation distribution"
)

plot_peptide_corr_distribution.corrDF(
    peptide_corr_df_corr,
    plot_title = "Corrected data: peptide correlation (from precomputed table)"
)

head(sample_corr_df_corr)
head(peptide_corr_df_corr)
```

## Feature-level diagnostics

Expectation: local run-order trends and batch jumps should flatten while
biological trajectories remain plausible.

```{r corr-feature-diagnostics, fig.cap="Post-correction feature-level diagnostics for selected peptides/proteins and control features.", fig.height=5.2}
plot_single_feature(
    feature_name = feature_id,
    df_long = pbf_corr,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    plot_title = sprintf("Corrected data: feature profile (%s)", feature_id)
)

plot_peptides_of_one_protein(
    protein_name = protein_name,
    peptide_annotation = peptide_annotation,
    protein_col = "Gene",
    df_long = pbf_corr,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    plot_title = sprintf("Corrected data: peptides from %s", protein_name)
)

plot_spike_in(
    spike_ins = spike_in_name,
    peptide_annotation = peptide_annotation,
    protein_col = "Gene",
    df_long = pbf_corr,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    plot_title = sprintf("Corrected data: spike-in profile (%s)", spike_in_name)
)

plot_iRT(
    irt_pattern = irt_pattern,
    peptide_annotation = peptide_annotation,
    protein_col = "ProteinName",
    df_long = pbf_corr,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    plot_title = sprintf("Corrected data: iRT profile (%s)", irt_pattern)
)

fit_df_corr <- adjust_batch_trend_df(
    pbf_corr,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    span = 0.7
)
fit_df_corr <- fit_df_corr[fit_df_corr$peptide_group_label %in% feature_id, ]

plot_with_fitting_curve(
    feature_name = feature_id,
    df_long = pbf_corr,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    fit_df = fit_df_corr,
    plot_title = sprintf("Corrected data: fitted trend (%s)", feature_id)
)
```

## QC summaries

Expectation: correction quality should be reflected jointly by CV,
classification metrics, intragroup variation, and outlier diagnostics.

```{r corr-qc-summaries, cache=TRUE, fig.cap="Post-correction QC summaries for variability, clustering agreement, intragroup consistency, and outlier/sub-batch detection.", fig.height=4.8}
cv_df_corr <- calculate_feature_CV(
    pbf_corr,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    biospecimen_id_col = "EarTag",
    unlog = FALSE
)

cv_df_corr_plot <- cv_df_corr %>%
    filter(is.finite(CV_total))

if (nrow(cv_df_corr_plot) == 0) {
    message("Skipping CV plots (corrected): no features with sufficient measurements.")
} else {
    plot_CV_distr.df(cv_df_corr_plot, batch_col = "MS_batch", plot_title = "Corrected data: CV distribution")
    plot_CV_distr.df(cv_df_corr_plot, batch_col = "MS_batch", plot_title = "CV distribution (limmaRBE)")
}

class_metrics_corr <- safe_classification_metrics(
    pbf_corr,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    known_col = "MS_batch"
)

intragroup_corr <- plot_intragroup_variation(
    pbf_corr,
    group_col = "Diet",
    fill_the_missing = -1,
    metrics = "correlation"
)

outliers_corr <- detect_outlier_samples(
    pbf_corr,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    n_pcs = 5
)

subbatches_corr <- subbatch_detection(
    pbf_corr,
    sample_annotation = sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    n_pcs = 5,
    method = "kmeans",
    k_max = 4
)

head(cv_df_corr)
class_metrics_corr
outliers_corr
subbatches_corr$summary
```

# Raw vs Corrected: Comparative Interpretation

This section focuses on direct before/after comparisons and practical
interpretation.

## Correlation distribution overlays

```{r compare-overlays, fig.cap="Overlay comparison of raw vs limmaRBE sample/peptide correlation distributions.", fig.height=4.8}
plot_sample_corr_distribution(
    list(raw = data_matrix_raw_log, limmaRBE = data_matrix_corrected),
    sample_annotation,
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    biospecimen_id_col = "EarTag",
    plot_param = "batch_replicate",
    plot_title = "Sample correlation (raw vs limmaRBE)"
)

plot_peptide_corr_distribution(
    list(raw = data_matrix_raw_log, limmaRBE = data_matrix_corrected),
    peptide_annotation,
    protein_col = "Gene",
    plot_title = "Peptide correlation (raw vs limmaRBE)"
)
```

Interpretation guideline:

- Higher within-biospecimen correlation across different batches is a
  positive sign.
- Reduced separation of purely batch-based pair types suggests technical
  correction success.

## Global structure comparison (PCA and stacked PVCA)

```{r compare-pca-pvca, fig.cap="Global before/after structure comparison via PCA and stacked PVCA summaries.", fig.height=4.8}
grid.arrange(pca_raw, pca_corr, ncol = 2)

plot_PVCA_stacked_from_saved(
    pvca_dir,
    plot_title = "PVCA stacked summary (raw vs limmaRBE)"
)
```

Interpretation guideline:

- PCA should show less separation by `MS_batch`.
- Stacked PVCA should show reduced technical contribution relative to
  raw.

## Key summary metrics table

```{r compare-key-metrics}
# Helpers to robustly summarize key diagnostics.
first_num <- function(x) {
    if (is.null(x) || !length(x)) {
        return(NA_real_)
    }
    suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x[[1]]))
}

pvca_share <- function(pvca_df, patterns) {
    if (is.null(pvca_df) || !all(c("label", "weights") %in% names(pvca_df))) {
        return(NA_real_)
    }
    hits <- Reduce(
        `|`,
        lapply(patterns, function(pat) grepl(pat, pvca_df$label, fixed = TRUE))
    )
    sum(pvca_df$weights[hits], na.rm = TRUE)
}

median_corr_by_group <- function(corr_df, group_label) {
    if (is.null(corr_df) || !all(c("batch_replicate", "correlation") %in% names(corr_df))) {
        return(NA_real_)
    }
    median(corr_df$correlation[corr_df$batch_replicate == group_label], na.rm = TRUE)
}

count_outliers <- function(x) {
    if (is.null(x)) {
        return(NA_real_)
    }
    if (is.data.frame(x) && "is_outlier" %in% names(x)) {
        return(sum(x$is_outlier, na.rm = TRUE))
    }
    if (is.list(x) && !is.null(x$outliers) && is.data.frame(x$outliers)) {
        return(nrow(x$outliers))
    }
    suppressWarnings(as.numeric(length(x)))
}

summary_metrics <- tibble::tibble(
    Metric = c(
        "PVCA technical share (MS_batch + digestion_batch)",
        "Median feature CV_total",
        "Median corr: same biospecimen / different batch",
        "Median corr: same batch / different biospecimen",
        "ARI for MS_batch labels (lower after correction is better)",
        "MCC for MS_batch labels (lower after correction is better)",
        "Silhouette for MS_batch labels (lower after correction is better)",
        "Detected outlier sample count"
    ),
    Raw = c(
        pvca_share(pvca_raw, c("MS_batch", "digestion_batch")),
        median(cv_df_raw$CV_total, na.rm = TRUE),
        median_corr_by_group(sample_corr_df_raw, "same_biospecimen\ndiff_batch"),
        median_corr_by_group(sample_corr_df_raw, "same_batch\ndiff_biospecimen"),
        first_num(class_metrics_raw$ARI),
        first_num(class_metrics_raw$MCC),
        first_num(class_metrics_raw$silhouette),
        count_outliers(outliers_raw)
    ),
    limmaRBE = c(
        pvca_share(pvca_corr, c("MS_batch", "digestion_batch")),
        median(cv_df_corr$CV_total, na.rm = TRUE),
        median_corr_by_group(sample_corr_df_corr, "same_biospecimen\ndiff_batch"),
        median_corr_by_group(sample_corr_df_corr, "same_batch\ndiff_biospecimen"),
        first_num(class_metrics_corr$ARI),
        first_num(class_metrics_corr$MCC),
        first_num(class_metrics_corr$silhouette),
        count_outliers(outliers_corr)
    )
) %>%
    mutate(Change = limmaRBE - Raw)

knitr::kable(
    summary_metrics,
    digits = 3,
    caption = "Key before/after metrics summarizing correction impact (raw vs limmaRBE)."
)
```

## Classification and distribution summaries

```{r compare-summaries, fig.cap="Tabular and split-violin summaries of classification and sample-correlation metrics before and after correction.", fig.height=4.8}
class_metrics_compare <- bind_rows(
    transform(class_metrics_raw, Step = "raw"),
    transform(class_metrics_corr, Step = "limmaRBE")
)
class_metrics_compare

corr_compare <- bind_rows(
    transform(sample_corr_df_raw, Step = "raw"),
    transform(sample_corr_df_corr, Step = "limmaRBE")
)

plot_split_violin_with_boxplot(
    corr_compare,
    y_col = "correlation",
    col_for_color = "Step",
    col_for_box = "batch_replicate",
    plot_title = "Sample correlation by replicate/batch (raw vs limmaRBE)"
)
```

# Best Practices and Recommendations

```{r best-practices-table, echo=FALSE}
best_practices <- tibble::tibble(
    Topic = c(
        "Recommended diagnostic order",
        "When to apply each diagnostic",
        "Selecting correction methods",
        "Computational considerations",
        "QC thresholds/guidelines",
        "Common pitfalls"
    ),
    Recommendation = c(
        "Start with metadata/design checks, then missingness/distributions, then structure (PCA/heatmap), then variance/correlation/QC metrics.",
        "Use missingness and distribution diagnostics early; use PVCA/variance partition for quantified attribution; use feature-level and QC metrics before final sign-off.",
        "Use limmaRBE for linear discrete effects with covariate control; use ComBat/mComBat for discrete offsets in complete matrices; use trend + discrete for run-order drift.",
        "Use `ProBatchFeatures` assays, subset for exploratory runs, cache expensive embedding/variance chunks, and only materialize heavy intermediate steps when needed.",
        "Prefer dataset-specific thresholds. Typical practical targets: lower technical PVCA share, lower batch-label ARI/MCC, improved replicate correlations, and stable/acceptable CV distributions.",
        "Do not correct before validating design; avoid interpreting a single metric in isolation; avoid aggressive correction that erases known biology; ensure covariates are encoded correctly."
    )
)

knitr::kable(
    best_practices,
    caption = "Practical recommendations for robust batch-effect diagnostics and correction selection."
)
```

<div class="alert alert-warning">
**Warning:** Lower batch-separability metrics are desirable only when
batch is technical. If batch is partly confounded with biology, combine
design checks with sensitivity analyses before deciding on correction
strength.
</div>

# Try It Yourself

## Adapt the workflow to your own data

```{r try-yourself-own-data, eval=FALSE}
# Replace example objects with your own data matrix and metadata.
pbf_user <- ProBatchFeatures(
    data_matrix = your_data_matrix,
    sample_annotation = your_sample_annotation,
    sample_id_col = "YourSampleIDColumn",
    name = "feature::raw"
)

# Typical first step: log-transform.
pbf_user <- pb_transform(
    pbf_user,
    from = "feature::raw",
    steps = "log2",
    store_fast_steps = TRUE
)
```

## Explore parameter variations

```{r try-yourself-parameter-variations, eval=FALSE}
# Compare embedding sensitivity.
plot_TSNE(pbf_log, color_by = "MS_batch", perplexity = 10, max_iter = 300)
plot_TSNE(pbf_log, color_by = "MS_batch", perplexity = 20, max_iter = 500)

# Compare UMAP settings.
plot_UMAP(pbf_log, color_by = "MS_batch", n_neighbors = 10, min_dist = 0.3)
plot_UMAP(pbf_log, color_by = "MS_batch", n_neighbors = 20, min_dist = 0.1)

# Evaluate alternative correction method (ComBat) when matrix is NA-free.
pbf_combat <- pb_transform(
    pbf_log,
    from = pb_current_assay(pbf_log),
    steps = "combat",
    params_list = list(list(batch_col = "MS_batch", covariates_cols = c("Diet", "Sex")))
)
```

## Customize plots and save outputs

```{r try-yourself-customize-save, eval=FALSE}
# Example: custom color scheme.
custom_colors <- sample_annotation_to_colors(
    pbf_log,
    factor_columns = c("MS_batch", "Diet"),
    numeric_columns = c("order")
)

plot_sample_mean(
    pbf_log,
    order_col = "order",
    batch_col = "MS_batch",
    color_by_batch = TRUE,
    color_scheme = custom_colors,
    plot_title = "Custom sample-mean plot"
)

# Save summary tables.
write.csv(summary_metrics, file = "batch_correction_summary_metrics.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Save a key figure.
ggsave("pca_raw_vs_limmaRBE.png", grid.arrange(pca_raw, pca_corr, ncol = 2), width = 10, height = 4)
```

# See Also

- Main package workflow vignette: `proBatch.Rmd`
- `ProBatchFeatures` vignette: `proBatchFeatures.Rmd`
- Correction APIs: `?pb_transform`, `?correct_batch_effects`,
  `?correct_with_removeBatchEffect`, `?correct_with_ComBat`,
  `?correct_with_mComBat`, `?center_feature_batch`,
  `?correct_with_BERT`, `?correct_with_PLSDA_batch`,
  `?correct_with_RUVIII_C`, `?correct_with_NormAE`,
  `?correct_with_omicsGMF`
- Design and QC diagnostics: `?validate_batch_design`,
  `?summarize_design`, `?calculate_classification_metrics`,
  `?detect_outlier_samples`, `?subbatch_detection`
- Related Bioconductor infrastructure: `QFeatures`,
  `SummarizedExperiment`, `limma`, `sva`, `variancePartition`, `pvca`

# Session Info

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
